title: Foundation model of neural activity predicts response to new stimulus types
methods: four-module ANN (perspective + modulation + ConvLSTM core + readout)
foundation pretraining on pooled multi-mouse neural recordings
transfer learning with frozen core and lightweight per-subject fine-tuning
out-of-distribution generalization evaluation (CC_norm across stimulus domains)
logistic regression on 512-d readout feature weights for anatomical prediction
parametric tuning analysis (OSI, DSI, SSI) in silico vs. in vivo
data_sources: In-house two-photon calcium imaging (14 mice, GCaMP6s, Baylor College of Medicine)
MICrONS serial electron microscopy anatomy (~60,000 neurons, 500M synapses)
core_conclusion: A foundation model trained on ~135,000 neurons from 14 mice generalizes with minimal fine-tuning to new mice and entirely new stimulus domains (coherent motion, noise, Gabor patches), and its readout feature weights serve as functional barcodes predicting anatomical cell types, dendritic morphology and synaptic connectivity in the MICrONS connectomics dataset.
why_it_matters: First foundation model of the sensory cortex, achieving radical data efficiency for new subjects; out-of-distribution generalization across qualitatively different stimulus classes; bridges neural function and nanoscale anatomy in a single unified model; enables unlimited in silico parametric experiments replacing costly in vivo work.
why_nature: Paradigm-defining neuroscience contribution analogous to what GPT did for NLP; cross-scale mechanistic insight connecting electrophysiology and nanoscale EM; published with landmark MICrONS companion papers; fully open model weights, training pipeline and datasets.
added_value_to_my_research: Foundation-core paradigm (freeze shared core, fit lightweight readout per subject) as blueprint for fine-tuning geospatial foundation models across data-sparse cities
Functional barcode (512-d readout weights) as template for spatial tract embeddings predicting out-of-distribution health outcomes without domain-specific labels
Modulation module (behavioral gating of core) mapped to socioeconomic-context modulation of spatial accessibility cores in 2SFCA/R2SFCA models
CC_norm cross-domain evaluation protocol as rigorous benchmark for spatial generalizability in explainable ML predictions of urban health access
Functional-barcode-beats-proximity principle applied to physics-informed graph learning: demand-profile node embeddings govern edge weights in EV charging efficiency-equity optimization

related.title: A foundation model for the Earth system
related.relation: parallel foundation-model paradigm applied to geophysical forecasting — same frozen-core + domain-adaptation structure

related.relation: foundation model approach extended to cognitive behavioral prediction — complementary to neural activity prediction

related.relation: directly related visual cortex modeling work — alternative compact architecture for predicting neural responses

related.title: Connectome-constrained networks predict neural activity across the fly visual system
related.relation: structure-constrained neural prediction analogous to the MICrONS functional barcode predictions of connectivity

related.title: Training data composition determines machine learning generalization and biological rule discovery
related.relation: training data diversity and out-of-distribution generalization principles directly relevant to the foundation model's stimulus-domain transfer

figures.caption: Four-module ANN architecture (perspective, modulation, core, readout) mapping visual stimuli and behavior to neural activity across visual cortex layers and areas
Predictive accuracy of per-session models vs. prior state-of-the-art and vs. training data volume — confirms 25-46% improvement and scaling-law behavior
Foundation models outperform individual models across 6 stimulus domains with substantially less training data, demonstrating out-of-distribution generalization
In vivo vs. in silico parametric tuning for ~30,000 neurons — median preferred orientation error 4° for strongly tuned cells
Readout feature weights (functional barcodes) predict visual area (68% acc.), cell type (32% acc.) and retinotopic organization in the MICrONS 1 mm3 EM volume
